title: Join external FastF1 lap-status and weather data as causal race-state features
summary: Next-lap pit calls are highly sensitive to Safety Car, VSC, yellow-flag, restart, and weather context, but the provided table does not expose those signals.
rationale: Next-lap pit calls are highly sensitive to Safety Car, VSC, yellow-flag, restart, and weather context, but the provided table does not expose those signals. FastF1 publicly exposes session status, track status, weather, and race-control data for modern seasons, which aligns with the 2022-2025 coverage here.
implementation_hint: Offline-join FastF1 race data by Year/Race/LapNumber/Driver and add only causal features available by lap end: current track status, previous-lap status transition, laps since SC/VSC start/end, current-lap weather, and incident/message counts. Keep a strict no-future-info audit around timing alignment.
expected_effect: Likely strongest on wet or interrupted races; may add orthogonal signal that hand-built tyre-life features cannot infer.
risk: Name/lap alignment errors can silently poison features; some status feeds may be recorded with slight timing jitter, so causal cutoff discipline matters.
